meta.mode: SSR/HYDRATION BLOCKER GOVERNANCE — analysis only, no runtime change, no wrapper, no centralization, no migration
meta.note: Blockers explain why H2.5 yielded 0 qualified candidates. Public-safe blockers carry a smallest additive fix pattern; all other buckets are observe-only. Crisis routing & business↔healing separation preserved.

ssrBlockerType: bare localStorage access without typeof-window guard
publicSafeBlockers.smallestFutureSafeFix: Default-initialize state to a deterministic value, then read storage in a post-mount useEffect under a `typeof window !== 'undefined'` guard (covers SSR + hydration in one pass).
publicSafeBlockers.reversibility: high (additive guard / two-pass init; revert by restoring original line or checkpoint)
publicSafeBlockers.migrationReadinessAfterFix: Becomes a qualified lowest-blast-radius stabilization candidate (H2.5 gates) once SSR + hydration guards are added.
publicSafeBlockers.governanceNotes: Public, non-sensitive: smallest-fix is additive and reversible. NO change made in H2.6.
